title: Ranking Keys, Stable Sorts, Ties, and Top-K Reports · Code Recall
summary: A delayed maintenance review reconstructs ranking keys, stable sorts, ties, and top-k reports from repository state, input bytes, decoded text, tokens, counters, ranking keys, report records, stdout, stderr, exit status, tests, package artifact, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for deterministic ranked-frequency report after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Declare primary count direction, secondary token collation, normalization, locale boundary, and exact tie policy.
Express mixed ascending and descending order with an explicit key or stable multi-pass sort and trace tuple comparison.
Use and test Python stable sorting when earlier order is intentionally retained within equal keys.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ranking Reproducibility
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Bookbot team handles case 1139 during an offline public-library reading report. Build deterministic Python evidence to rank all eligible words with explicit ties and reproducible top-k output; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare repeated and partitioned runs byte-for-byte and explain any locale, Unicode, or policy dependency.

Inspect the Ranking Reproducibility trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ranking Reproducibility.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Ranking Reproducibility
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Ranking Reproducibility: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Bookbot team handles case 5664 during a digital-humanities corpus revision. Build deterministic Python evidence to rank all eligible words with explicit ties and reproducible top-k output; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare repeated and partitioned runs byte-for-byte and explain any locale, Unicode, or policy dependency.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Ranking Reproducibility.
steps.why: Retrieving Ranking Reproducibility through the concrete deterministic ranked-frequency report case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Ranking Reproducibility, keep Ranking Reproducibility connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare repeated and partitioned runs byte-for-byte and explain any locale, Unicode, or policy dependency.

Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare repeated and partitioned runs byte-for-byte and explain any locale, Unicode, or policy dependency.

For Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Ranking Reproducibility, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Deterministic output matters only to automated tests, not users.
